target.name: Axibo AI
target.founder: Anoop Gadhrri, Sohaib Al-Emara, Reiner Schmidt

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised C$12M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 firms: one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. While seven same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, none satisfy the filtering criteria for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation-tier similarity required for a defensible median. Applying an all-sector median would introduce uncontrolled heterogeneity and produce a misleading result. The absence of sufficiently comparable seed-stage robotics companies in the available dataset prevents triangulation via this method.

Given the methodological constraints, a valuation range cannot be reliably established from the data provided. The analysis is constrained by the sparsity of directly comparable seed-stage robotics firms. To support a quantitative valuation, additional data on revenue multiples, funding-round pricing, or stage-specific discount rates within the robotics subsector would be required. The key uncertainty is whether the broader public and private peer set can be decomposed into meaningful stage cohorts; without this segmentation, applying median multiples risks anchoring to companies with fundamentally different capital structures and cash-generation profiles than an early-stage venture.
